On-screen text reads "And it did write..." followed by "poorly...".
evidence_entries.inference_hint: This directly characterizes the first blue-pen writing attempt as poor handwriting rather than a neat word, a picture with seams, or no writing.

evidence_entries.evidence_text: A later scene shows the robot moving on a board in a darker setting and producing a drawing that looks like a fish or creature. This is separated from the earlier first blue-pen writing attempt.
evidence_entries.inference_hint: Do not map the later picture-drawing scene to the answer about the first blue-pen drawing.

In that first attempt, Scrubby does make blue marks, but they appear as a messy crude 'H' or 'I' rather than neat writing.
The on-screen description says it wrote "poorly," which matches poor handwriting and rules out neat writing, a later picture, or no writing at all.

trace_package.final_answer: A. The handwriting is very poor.

The robot's eyes glow purple as it writes. The scene then cuts to a different robot with a similar design, but with glowing green eyes, writing on a different whiteboard.

transcript_segments.text: We always forget to clean the whiteboard, so we build a robot, Scrubby. Scrubby doesn't just erase the board, it also writes meeting templates, and after work, it enjoys drawing. We made it because after every meeting in the studio, no one remembers to wipe the board, and by the time we meet again, it was hard to wipe off.

So he figured he might not get a colleague he never forgets. His chassis has two wheels. It's driven by a pair of tiny motors. Together, they power the wheels. A single servo controls steering, so it can turn. And this big magnet that keeps it stuck to the board. But Scrubby can only look ahead. How does it know where to cling? That's where his buddy Cammy comes in.

Cammy's left eye is the camera that sees writing. The right eye is infrared and tracks Scrubby. Together, they guide Scrubby to the writing. Now after every meeting, Scrubby automatically wipes the board. When it's tired, it heads over to the stock for wireless charging. That was already pretty good until we showed it to an investor. Maybe just put a market on it so that it can write too.

captions.visual: A person holds a small, beige robot shaped like a cassette tape. The robot has large cartoon eyes and a yellow beak. It uses a blue marker to draw a zigzag line on a white surface. The text overlay reads, 'That's because its arm had so little flexibility, it had to use its whole body to write, like brushing your teeth.'

shooting_style: The segment uses a quick cut from a medium shot to a top-down view to emphasize the robot's drawing action.

transcript_segments.text: So we swapped the eraser for a pen, and it did write poorly. That's because its arm has so little flexibility, it had to move its whole body to write, like brushing your teeth with moving your head. So we upgraded Scrubby's arm. The new one has two joints, way more flexible. Now we can write a whole character within and go to the next box and repeat until it finishes the sentence.
We even added a tiny fan to keep it cool while working. We thought about making a tick-mating nose too, but at this speed, just let it goof off. We'll help his buddy Gummy practice shooting.

But hey, if they can write, could they draw? Our first idea was to divide an image into small sections, have Scrubby draw one, then shift to the next. But that left obvious seams between sections. We tried dot art, but you couldn't tell what it was. So we gave it a few tethers, letting it run around the board with a marker. There are plenty of world drawing robots, but most just use one color. So we made this, an auto-rotating marker dot.

Scrubby can swap markers and draw in color. These are not vivid enough, so we fill the pens with fluorescent ink, shine a UV light on it, and it glows. Now when the studio winds down at night, you might catch a scene like this.

captions.visual: A small, green, rectangular robot with large, glowing green eyes and cartoonish eyelashes is mounted on a wooden block. It is positioned in front of a large whiteboard. A yellow lightning bolt icon with a 'V' inside is attached to the robot, and a small green light on a red and white striped pole is visible to the left.
